mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for local-first deployment on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks including text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a self-hosted solution with a focus on privacy and control.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing your LocalAI deployment.

```bash

curl -Lo localai https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai

sudo mv localai /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the LocalAI binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development and testing.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI to your Kubernetes cluster using a Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI in the localai namespace.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks that the LocalAI pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the localai namespace to verify deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the LocalAI deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the LocalAI pods.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall the LocalAI Helm release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This step will upgrade LocalAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp localai-pod:/path/to/data /local/backup

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Backup data from the LocalAI pod.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the LocalAI pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs localai-pod -n localai' and adjust resource limits or configurations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Docker image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in the Helm chart values, and ensure your cluster can access the image repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to LocalAI service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI service is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the service status with 'kubectl get svc -n localai' and ensure the LocalAI pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for LocalAI.

**Fix:**
Increase the CPU and memory limits in the Helm chart values and redeploy.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved API endpoints for better performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy model support; users should migrate to the new model formats.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.10.x, ensure to update your model configurations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI service running with the command 'kubectl get svc -n localai' showing the service endpoints.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
